sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Santa Clara, sitting at 6th in the league with 51 points, host FC Arouca, who are positioned 12th with 34 points, in Round 31 at the Estadio de São Miguel in Ponta Delgada. This matchup promises to be intriguing given the disparity in league standings. The referee for this game, Ricardo Baixinho, is known for a high average of 6.3 yellow cards per game and 0.48 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter. With Santa Clara looking to solidify their upper mid-table spot and FC Arouca aiming to climb away from the lower half, both teams have plenty to play for.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Historically, FC Arouca have dominated this fixture, securing 4 wins in the last 5 matchups against Santa Clara, with 1 draw and no wins for the home side. Their most recent encounter on December 15, 2024, saw FC Arouca triumph 1-0 at home, with Jason earning the Player of the Match title with a Sofascore rating of 7.7. Additionally, FC Arouca have been the first to score in 4 of the last 5 meetings, and both teams have scored in 5 of the last 7 games. Head-to-head trends also show a tendency for high card counts, with more than 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 6 matches, and a consistent pattern of fewer than 10.5 corners in 6 of the last 7 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Santa Clara’s Form and Key Players
sections.text: Santa Clara come into this game with a streak of 3 matches without a win and 3 without a clean sheet, though they have consistently played in low-scoring games, with under 2.5 goals in their last 9 home matches. Their attack is led by Vinícius Lopes, a forward with 8 goals and an expected goals (xG) value of 8.03, showing clinical finishing. Gabriel Silva, another forward, stands out with a league rank of 3 for times fouled per game at 2.6, often drawing attention from defenders, and has contributed 5 goals and 3 assists. Defensively, Sidney Lima has chipped in with 3 goals and 1 assist, unusual for a defender, while Matheus Pereira adds creativity from the back with 4 assists. Santa Clara rank 5th in the league for fewest goals conceded (30) and 3rd for overperformance against expected goals (+6.3897), showcasing defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: FC Arouca’s Form and Standout Performers
sections.text: FC Arouca have had mixed results recently, with both teams scoring in 4 of their last 5 away games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities but also attacking intent. Leading their line is Henrique Araújo, a forward with 5 goals and an xG of 5.77, supported by Alfonso Trezza and Güven Yalçın, each with 4 goals. Defensively, Boris Popović stands out with a league rank of 2 for ground duels won at 66.7%, a crucial asset in midfield battles. Lamba Chico, another defender, has contributed offensively with 1 goal and 2 assists, adding versatility to their backline. Their matches tend to see fewer than 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 7 games and under 10.5 corners in 6 of the last 7 away fixtures, suggesting a more controlled style of play.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Statistics
sections.text: Santa Clara have shown strength in creating big chances, ranking 6th in the league with 67, though their conversion rate is poor at 23.88%, ranking 18th. They excel in late-game scoring, with 23.33% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, and also between the 46th and 60th minutes. However, they commit the most fouls in the league (522, rank 1) and have received the most yellow cards (102, rank 18), averaging 3.1875 per game, which could be a concern against a strict referee. FC Arouca, on the other hand, also peak late, scoring 23.4% of their goals between the 76th and 90th minutes and 17% between the 61st and 75th minutes, hinting at a strong finish. Both teams average low corner counts, with Santa Clara at 3.8125 per game (rank 13), aligning with head-to-head trends of under 10.5 corners.

sections.subtitle: Notable Past Performers and Venue Factor
sections.text: Luís Rocha of Santa Clara has been a standout in past matchups against FC Arouca, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.27 over 2 starts, though he hasn’t contributed goals or assists. Playing at Estadio de São Miguel could give Santa Clara an edge, as home advantage often plays a role, despite their recent inability to keep clean sheets in 7 consecutive home games against FC Arouca. The venue’s atmosphere in Ponta Delgada could inspire players like Ricardinho, who has overperformed his xG by 2.9607 with 7 goals, to push for a result. For FC Arouca, maintaining their historical edge will rely on key players like Morlaye Sylla, who despite underperforming his xG of 5.55 with just 2 goals, remains a creative force in midfield.
